The '''pulse-wave blaster''', or '''pulse-wave pistol''', was a ranged weapon seen as the predecessor of the [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. They were commonly in use c. [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], but had been replaced by the first models of the standard blaster by [[3956 BBY]]. By the time of the [[Rise of the Empire era]] these weapons were virtually extinct and considered archaic, however they were still used in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], particularly on backwater worlds.<ref name="Arms & Equipment Guide">''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]''</ref>

==Description==
Pulse-wave [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] operated with similar principles to a regular blaster, firing small packets of coherent energy at a target. The energy dissipated as it moved away from the weapon making it largely unsuited for long ranged engagements. This weapon, however, created a spatial distortion when fired, which altered atomic make-up and was thus deadly at close range. This effect was much like that of [[Disruptor/Legends|disruptor]] weapons, making even the most ancient of models that existed in the modern era illegal for civilian use. Despite this, pulse-wave blasters were generally less powerful than their [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] successors.

The standard pulse-wave pistol cost 200 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. It weighed 1 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]] and fired 100 shots before its [[Power cell/Legends|power pack]] needed to be replaced.

===Variants===
Variants of this weapon included the [[ATA Pulse-wave Blaster]] produced by [[Greff-Timms Industrial/Legends|Greff-Timms Industrial]], the [[B-200 Pulse-Wave Needler]], the [[C-202 pulse-wave blaster]], the [[R-113 Pulse-Wave Blaster]], and the [[U-13 Pulse-Wave Blaster]].

A larger, [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] version of this weapon was the [[pulse-wave rifle]].

==History==
The [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droids]] created by the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] were equipped with pulse-wave blasters as their ranged weapon.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> The [[Charr Ontee]] soldiers of [[Kathol (planet)|Kathol]] used pulse-wave weaponry.<ref name="Endgame">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>
